codeReview.summary: The support matrix and warning+omit handling were implemented correctly for non-interactive/all flows, but interactive `install` with `--agent` bypasses unsupported-combination filtering for hooks/subagents, so unsupported work can still execute without warning.

issues.description: `install` routes non-`-y` runs directly to `interactive(undefined, targets)` without applying category support checks. In this path, unsupported artifact-target combinations are not filtered before execution.

`runInstall` always executes `installHooks`/`installSubagents` when selected, regardless of `targets`. Because `needsTargetPicker` only applies to skills/rules (line 85), `af install --agent codexcli` in TTY can still install hooks/subagents (unsupported) with no warning+omit behavior, violating VAL-SCOPE-004/005 intent for unsupported combinations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure requires manual verification of interactive and non-TTY flows, but the handoff evidence only lists non-interactive command runs while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is marked true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-03-03T18-24-50-957Z__install-scoping-unsupported-combinations-policy__42c89898-c17f-4031-8b95-0b9ddb4ec30b.json -> verification.commandsRun contains only `-y`/non-interactive runs; no interactive-check evidence is recorded.

summary: FAIL: A blocking gap remains in interactive install flows. Unsupported combos are warned+omitted in the newly covered non-interactive paths, but `install` without `-y` can still execute unsupported hooks/subagents for explicit unsupported agents.
